description: Irish referendum to repeal the Eighth Amendment (abortion ban) from the constitution. YES (Repeal) won 66.4% vs NO (Retain) 33.6% with 64.1% turnout. Tests social liberalization trend with decisive outcome despite initial uncertainty.

key_events.description: Citizens' Assembly recommends repeal with no gestational limits. Government announces referendum for May 2018.
Campaign intensifies. Save the 8th uses graphic imagery; Together for Yes focuses on compassionate personal stories (Savita Halappanavar).
Polls narrow significantly in mid-campaign. NO side gains momentum with 'hard cases' arguments and claims of UK-style abortion-on-demand.
Final weeks see strong YES rebound. Personal testimonials from Irish women under #InHerShoes campaign go viral on social media.
Home to Vote movement: Irish diaspora flies home to vote. Social media amplifies emotional urgency. Final polls show clear YES lead.

calibration_notes: Tests social liberalization referendum with mid-campaign volatility. Polls initially showed solid YES lead, then narrowed dramatically in weeks 3-4 of campaign (echoing 2015 marriage equality pattern), before YES surged to a much larger final margin than polls predicted. Key dynamics: shy YES voters in conservative Ireland, personal narrative campaign effectiveness vs graphic shock tactics, diaspora mobilization, generational divide, and the 'Savita effect' as sustained emotional anchor for the YES campaign.
